paper_title: Development of a dielectric ceramic based on diatomite-titania. Part one: powder preparation and sintering study

methods.description: Process for preparing and sintering mixtures of titania and diatomite to study their properties.

steps.description: Samples were prepared by sintering uniaxial pressed mixtures (200 MPa) of titania with 0,25 (D25), 50 (D50), 75 (D75) and 100 (D100) wt % of diatomite.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare samples by pressing and sintering mixtures of titania and diatomite

steps.description: The powder was prepared by calcination (900 o C), milling and sieving (400 mesh).
The material was sintered in air at temperatures between 1200 o C and 1500 o C for 0.5 to 48 h.
steps.searchable_summary: sinter material at specified temperatures and durations

methods.method_name: Sintering Constant and Activation Energy Calculation
methods.description: Calculation of the sintering constant and activation energy using specific formulas.

steps.description: The sintering constant (k) and the activation energy (Q) were calculated respectively by [1]: where T and t are respectively the sintering temperature and time; the indexes 1 and 2 refer to different conditions, R is the gas constant and (∆L/L o ) 1 and (∆L/L o ) 2 are the linear shrinkage of two different samples.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate sintering constant and activation energy

methods.description: Use of X-ray diffraction to identify phases in the sintered material.

steps.description: X-ray diffraction analyses were made with CuK α radiation for phase identification.
steps.searchable_summary: perform X-ray diffraction analysis for phase identification

methods.description: Analysis of the microstructure of specimens using scanning electron microscopy.

steps.description: The microstructures of specimens were analyzed by scanning electron microscopy (SEM).

methods.method_name: Mass Variation and Linear Shrinkage Measurement
methods.description: Measurement of mass variation and linear shrinkage using standard expressions.

steps.description: The mass variation and the linear shrinkage were measured using the usual expressions.
steps.searchable_summary: measure mass variation and linear shrinkage
